name: Caramel Surprise Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Freeze caramel candies until ready to use.
2. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
3. Generously grease and flour eight 1-cup ramekins, placing them on a baking sheet.
4. Chop chocolate into small pieces.
5. Melt chocolate with butter in a double boiler over simmering water. Remove from heat and stir until smooth. Allow to cool.
6. Beat eggs, egg yolks, and sugar together for about 6 minutes using high speed. The mixture should turn pale yellow and become thicker.
7. Fold the cooled chocolate mixture and vanilla into the batter. Sift flour over the chocolate mixture and fold it in gently.
8. Pour batter evenly into ramekins.
9. Bake for about 7-8 minutes until set on top but still jiggly on bottom.
10. Remove from oven and insert two frozen caramel candies into the center of half the cakes. Return to oven and bake another 7-8 minutes until caramel candies are melted.
11. Cool ramekins on a wire rack for about 10 minutes.
12. Chop chocolate into pieces.
13. Heat half the cream with sugar in a saucepan over medium heat until it boils, then remove from heat and stir in chocolate until smooth.
14. Add remaining cream to the pan and stir until fully combined.
15. Warm the jar of Caramel sauce separately.
16. Pour caramel sauce into dessert plates, creating a pool at the bottom of each.
17. Drizzle chocolate sauce over the caramel sauce in zigzag patterns, adding feathering with a toothpick if desired.
18. Loosen cakes with a sharp knife and invert onto the sauces.
19. Top with additional chocolate sauce and serve.
THE DESSERT SHOW SHOW #DS3060
